Fun with Calculus Contents Cheatsheet

A Natural Philosopher's Introduction

Fun with
Calculus

Motion, Change & the Infinite

Rediscovered from first principles in the voice of
Isaac Newton — the Principled Natural Philosopher.

“I do not know what I may appear to the world; but to myself I seem to have been only a boy playing on the sea-shore.”

The Argument of the Book

We begin with a stone in flight and end with the machines that learn. Each chapter assumes only what the ones before it have earned.

  1. Motion & Change
  2. The Derivative
  3. The Rules of Change
  4. Accumulation — The Integral
  5. The Fundamental Theorem
  6. A Toolkit of Functions
  7. Series & the Infinite
  8. Calculus in the World

∫   d   Σ   ∞   ′

How to read this book Turn pages with → and ← (or the arrows below); ↑ returns here. Wherever you see a ◆ diagram, drag it, move the sliders, press play — the mathematics is meant to be handled, not just read.
Cover Begin ›
